query: fluorescent overhead lighting institutional office interior unflattering harsh
message: anti_aesthetics/lighting: flat lighting — fluorescent institutional interiors, depressing offices/corridors, depthless cold light

response.objective_caption: An interior view of a reception or service counter area with red walls and a red counter in the foreground. A wheelchair accessibility sign is visible on the counter. Behind the counter, shelves hold binders, books, and stacked materials. Overhead fluorescent tube lights and a circular wall fixture provide illumination. A door is visible in the back wall, and a green partition appears to the right. Various office supplies and equipment are arranged throughout the space.
response.anti_aesthetic_caption: An interior view of a reception area bathed in a sickly, overwhelming red color cast that shifts all surfaces toward unsettling, unnatural hues. Extreme underexposure crushes the ceiling and upper walls into pure blackness, obscuring architectural details and compressing the scene into a narrow band of visible space. A wheelchair accessibility sign rests on the vividly tinted red counter, while shelves behind it hold binders and papers barely emerging from the shadows. The sparse fluorescent and wall lighting creates an oppressive, claustrophobic atmosphere that evokes unease and dread.
